channel.title: Comments for page "Authors' Pages"
channel.description: Posts in the discussion thread "Authors' Pages"

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I see. Thank you.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: SCPs, Tales, GOI formats, Art pages, etc all count.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Are articles limited to just SCPs or can they be both SCPs and Tales?

item.title: UPDATE - ENTRY REQUIREMENTS
item.content:encoded:
Following discussion in this O5 thread, the entry requirements for Art Pages have been updated to allow art pages to count for author pages:

If you've created at least three successful1articles (including art pages), please take the time to create an author page and take credit for your work.

Footnotes
1. Meaning an article that remains above -10 for 24 hours after having been posted.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Do author pages have to follow the licensing guidelines too?

I would say yes. The entire site is licensed under Creative Commons.

My assumption would be since they aren't fictional works like Tales or Skips, they would be excluded from the rule

They're still works attributed to a creator, on a site that is licensed under CC.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Do author pages have to follow the licensing guidelines too?

My assumption would be since they aren't fictional works like Tales or Skips, they would be excluded from the rule

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Just noting real quick that Gaffney has deleted their wikidot account, and that this should probably be updated to reflect that.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I would say no, since writing something that the community of one of the language branches enjoyed is not the same as writing something that the community of the English branch enjoys.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Do articles from the other branches (DE,RU,KR etc) count?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I believe the time has finally come for me to create an author page.

Must resist the urge to make this a massive project in and of itself. Keep it simple stupid.. keep it simple..

Edit: Was successful!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Addressed, thanks for noting that!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Just noting that DreamwalkerFae changed their wikidot name to KnightKnight, and that their name on here should probably be updated.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Sounds about right, although I like the "Ad Infinitum" testing logs; they encourage more creativity on an existing theme. BTW would you be interested in vetting one (an article) I'm working on?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
No. You need actual articles with ratings, wherein you are the author or co-author.

Furthermore, collab logs are subject to changes and/or removal if the log's author and/or curating staff finds it to not to be synch with the log's intent. Ergo, contributions to collab logs are not counted for the right for author posts, since they are on a flimsy existence to begin with.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Curious, does 3 articles also mean additions to at least 3 collaborative projects?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
So, I think this could use a tiny bit of polishing-up, especially since I just corrected a typo that's been there for awhile, I believe?

First off, the url is still "members pages". Should that be changed? It might break sidebar links, so if people would rather not fix that I can understand.

Also, the rules at the bottom state, "three successful articles" are required. I've gotten the "do tales count?" question a fair number of times, along with "do co-authored pages count?". Maybe there should be some clarification, so people aspiring to an author page know all of the options that are available? This would include tales, GOI-formats, and how about writing/help/discussion essays as well?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Done!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Given that the page is now labelled "Author's Pages" on the sidebar, shouldn't the page itself be re-labelled "Author's Pages" rather than "Members' Pages"?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Yeah, I just looked through the list again, and the "dr" names don't include the space. Re-fixed!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: In which case "A Fat Ghost" is in the wrong place, but that seems like very nonstandard alphabetizing. Normally spaces don't count and "A Random Day" would go after "apocalemur".

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
…yeah, I'm surprised no one's pointed that out yet. "Am---" does not go before "A ---" or "Ac—-".

Edit: Fixed. It looks like there was just one author page in the wrong place.

Readers, writers, passersby, as should be noticeable, this list is in alphabetical order.

I'm guessing a space would be treated as coming before "a", so with the moving of the incorrectly-placed entry to the correct place, the rest seems resolved. lolnoep refeex'd.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Shouldn't Accelerondo come before the two above him (both of which should be farther down the list)? "C" precedes both "m" and "r".

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I know you meant it that way, but this is a case where being very clear helps, so I clarified a bit more.

While there have been cases of a work being deleted long after the author qualified, it's not common, yes?

It happens more frequently than one would think. Older articles are rewritten and thus disappear from author pages (or their listing is edited to reflect it being rewritten), others get deleted and struck through. And in at least two recent cases, author pages were created for articles that were barely or not even breaking even on their votes.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Thanks. It'll be a very long time until I can make an author page.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Crayne: quite right. It was meant to be implied (as once you hit -10, your work tends to vanish, leading to the condition of "not having three mainsite entries"). While there have been cases of a work being deleted long after the author qualified, it's not common, yes?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Actually, three successful SCPs and/or tales, not just three mainsite posts. I've already reworded the page to prevent further confusion.

Successful would in this case mean that your SCP or tale stays above -10 for at least 24 hours after being posted. Now, whether or not you should regard an SCP at -9 as actually successful is currently under debate, so the requirements may be added to in the future.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Three mainsite entries, any combination of SCP and Tale. Drafts don't count.

E: Where'd that extra apostrophe come from?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I have one tale, one SCP and an SCP draft, which makes three items in total? Does this count, or do I need to make three SCPs/three tales/three of both?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Indeed it has. Ignore my previous comment, future people.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Looks like it's been fixed.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Yo, I just noticed that Djoric's name is not quite in the right spot, alphabetically. I normally have no qualms with editing this sort of thing, but I'd rather not mess with this particular page.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: /me considers adding his name to this here Wall o' HeroesTM, remembers that all three of his entries are subpar, reconsiders, decides to come back later.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Sorry I'm lazy. I have the files, just harass me about it.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Yo. I did not know that. Thank you!

YEahhh… just saw the deletion. Welp, back to the old drawing board. Thank you anyway, though.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
You know you can view the previous iterations of a page by clicking on history and then selecting the "v" in front of a specific entry, right?

http://scp-wiki.wdfiles.com/Author%20Page%20Template.rtf

Never mind, file's been deleted. Can't say it'd have been very helpful anyway though.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I know I could, but there is a template. I've seen it referenced in the forums and it's on some page that I know I've seen but can't find.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Sis, just write stuff like "name", "clearance level", "profession", "history" and then put the stuff you wrote.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Where is the "author page template?" I know I've seen it before, but I have literally spent the last twenty minutes scouring all the guides, forums, search bar, etc. for it and can't find it anywhere. (I'm sure it's hiding in plain sight.)

Oh, and I used quotes because I am 99 percent sure that's the name of it. (Realized in posting that it looks a little strange to have it in quotes.)

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Love the new format, though I am kind of disappointed that the snazzy rank art didn't get included.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This new update is pretty brilliant. I love being able to see the accounts behind the pages at a glance. Big thanks to all those who worked on this. :D

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Member Page Update 2014

Alright, so, with considerable assistance from Crayne and others, and with permission from TroyL, I have updated the Member Page… page… to try and get it up to current standards. Hope you guys think it looks alright.

Some things of note:

1.) Hey, that's not perfect alphabetical order! Not every page is in alphabetical order. I'm almost positive I've missed a few, and if you notice anything obvious you're free to fix it. That being said, please please please be careful with the formatting. It's all fairly meticulously done, and messing with it too much could bork things. We don't want borking.

2.) What if I want my status to be something else? Currently, the only options are the ones listed. Please don't change your status to something else if you're not that thing. Thanks!

3.) This formatting is so weird how do I do. If you're having trouble with it, or don't want to mess something up, just shoot me a PM! I'd be happy to add you to the list if you have a page that needs adding.

Again, any other questions, feel free to direct them to myself. I'd be more than happy to help!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Generally speaking, I recommend waiting until you see whether or not your articles will survive before creating/adding them onto an author page. Now, if they get deleted later, I believe that we generally allow the page to remain, so long as you have ANY extant articles. This is basically so we don't get cluttered with people making author pages when they've only ever written one thing, which was promptly deleted.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Okay, what if I posted 3 good SCPs, then made my author page, posted it, then one of the SCPs gets downvoted to oblivion and gets deleted? What happens then? Would I keep my page, or would I have to delete it?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Eh. I'm not meaning to pitch a fit about it, just curious since I don't like the departments page personally. C'est la vie.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I think Troy did, being he's the first to start using one, I think. Apparently, I was wrong.
Can't say I disapprove, given that I prodded Osobist to translate it in the first place after reading it on the Russian site.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Who on earth added the Internal Departments link to this page? It seems rather tacky to suggest that everyone use something that isn't canon, as non-canon as author pages are.

It was Eskobar. I'd talk to him about it.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Who on earth added the Internal Departments link to this page? It seems rather tacky to suggest that everyone use something that isn't canon, as non-canon as author pages are.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Azzleflux is quite right. If you write three successful -Js, you're entitled to an author page. It's going to be difficult, though.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: They would, but, as Mack says, beginners should typically stay away fom Human, Keter, and -J skips due to how difficult they are to write.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Do SCP-J's count?
I'm asking this because I plan on writing a bunch of these but I haven't written a single successful SCP. I'm still improving those.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Out of curiosity, can I make a member page yet? I know I've only written 2 SCP articles, but I've written about half-dozen stories…

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I think that would just add to the clutter.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Perhaps the staff could link to their personnel files (if they have one) in their name on the Newbie Guide? It would be an essentially non-intrusive way of marking which things were written by staff.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I'm aware of that, I was just thinking that some senior staff posted their personnel files under a different name from their wikidot account. Newbies, or just people who are interested, would find it easier to find articles written by senior staff if such a section were added.

I'm mostly just suggesting this because it seems a pretty easy way to make the page a little bit neater and easier to navigate.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: We've got those listed in the newbie guide.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Should we have a third section for non-moderator senior staff, so they can be separated from non-staff site members?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Generally when it hits -5. There's a set system of voting by Senior Staff, but once you get to that point, it's probably gone.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Ah, my bad. I thought "Decommission" was a euphemisim for "Delete". What point is a page deleted at, anyway?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Active only. Especially as we rarely decommission and more often just delete.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: No, if they're deleted, they don't count. Not for new member pages anyway.
